channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump will stand before Congress and offer a divided US an accounting of his turbulent first weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday is expected to lay out for Congress and the American people his plans for the weeks and months ahead after a turbulent first few weeks in office, which have featured a dismantling of the federal government, tensions with America’s allies and a trad
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration is set to drop lawsuit pushing Idaho to allow emergency abortions, filing shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration signaled Tuesday it will let Idaho enforce its strict abortion ban in the treatment of pregnant women suffering medical emergencies, a dramatic reversal from the previous administration in a closely watched lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas lawmakers scold juvenile justice department over scathing DOJ report
item.description: Democratic state Rep. Gene Wu criticized the department for treating an August 2024 federal report as “nothing.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: The IRS is drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to try to cut its workforce of roughly 90,000 people in half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation and not authorized to speak on the record.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers reveal top issues they want Trump to hammer home during joint address to Congress
item.description: Members of Congress told Fox News Digital on Capitol Hill what they hope to hear from President Donald Trump during his joint address to Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In polarized times, these Minnesota legislators want to restore civility at the state Capitol
item.description: This year’s legislative session started with a lot of vitriol between DFLers and Republicans. But a bipartisan group of legislators is trying to get both sides to be more collaborative and civil.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Demonstrators across 50 states look to unify a disparate opposition to Trump and his sweeping agenda
item.description: As Donald Trump prepares to address a joint session of Congress, protest groups are gathering at parks, statehouses and other public grounds to assail his presidency as dangerous and un-American.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Charlotte Kramon And Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department is reviewing prosecution of Colorado clerk who supported Trump’s election lies
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The Department of Justice is backing a former county election clerk in Colorado who was convicted for her role in allowing supporters of President Donald Trump to access confidential data about the 2020 election, the latest move by the administration to use its power to reward allies w
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINA CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota farmers fret about ‘chaos and uncertainty’ following latest Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota producers say they’re worried about the impact that tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico coupled with retaliatory tariffs could have on the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: President Trump to address a Joint Session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump delivers an address to a Joint Session of Congress on March 4 to outline his vision and priorities for his administration’s first year. Watch NPR’s live Special Coverage.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of Mexico's lawsuit against American gunmakers
item.description: Mexico argues it should be allowed to sue American gunmakers for their weapons being smuggled into the country and used by cartels, but the U.S. Supreme Court appears skeptical.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem says DHS will 'not be deterred' after ICE hit by new leaks ahead of Virginia raid
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem is responding to leaks about a raid in Northern Virginia, after officials have said they are trying to crack down on leaks to the media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: Fifteen individuals will sit with the first lady, with the invited individuals intended to highlight Trump's accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge reverses Trump firing of employee appeals board member
item.description: A federal judge ruled Tuesday that the Trump administration’s firing of a federal employee appeals board member was unlawful. U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras ruled in favor of Cathy Harris, who chaired the three-person Merit Systems Protection Board (MSPB) during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP rebels fire warning shot in shutdown showdown: No DOGE, no deal
item.description: Conservatives in Congress are urging GOP leadership in both chambers not to agree to limiting DOGE in a spending bill, as Democrats are asking for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to make 'full-throated' case during primetime speech: former presidential speechwriters
item.description: As President Donald Trump delivers his first major address to a joint session of Congress since starting his second tour of duty in the White House, Democrat and Republican former presidential speechwriters offer advice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pauses US military aid to Ukraine while pressuring Zelenskyy to move toward quick end to war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in negotiations to end the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms McMahon to lead Education Department as Trump pushes to shut it down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted Monday to confirm former wrestling executive Linda McMahon as the nation’s education chief, a role that places her atop a department that President Donald Trump has vilified and vowed to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 3 things in Minnesota that will be impacted by tariffs on Canada
item.description: Tariffs from the Trump administration are set to go in effect on Tuesday. Canada and Minnesota do over $21 billion in trade every year.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Ellen Finn, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance's role in Zelenskyy clash puts spotlight on VP
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's vocal involvement was among several noteworthy moments and led to mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico makes case to avoid U.S. tariffs as it awaits Trump’s decision
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum has said that her administration is waiting to see if U.S. President Donald Trump makes good on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on Mexican imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Waltz likens Zelenskyy to an 'ex-girlfriend' after Oval Office spat
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz compared Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to an “ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Rep. Byron Donalds, backed by Trump, says he's running for Florida governor
item.description: Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds announced Tuesday that he's entering the 2026 race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, who is ineligible to seek another term after eight years in office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
